relations.note: Gold/Sunshine restriction is a concrete application of the general contrast testing requirement.

quotes: WCAG AA — 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text ≥24px or 19px bold
WCAG AA — 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text ≥24px or 19px bold.

rules.intent: Guarantee legal readability and accessibility compliance across all text/background combinations.
rules.rule_text: Every text/background pair must be tested for contrast (via webaim.com or equivalent) against the baseline WCAG AA minimums: 4.5:1 for body text and 3:1 for large text (≥24px, or ≥19px bold).

effect.reserved_for: surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only
effect.subject: Gold (#ffc60a) / Sunshine (#faa31b) text

quotes: golds are for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only
Known-safe pairs: Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy.
Avoid: Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast) — golds are for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

rules.intent: Prevent inaccessible gold-on-white text usage while codifying the brand's proven-safe color pairings.
rules.rule_text: Known-safe text/background pairs are Brand Blue (#00529b), Deep Blue (#003D74), Dark Navy (#011E45), or Graphite (#212121) on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand, and White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy. Gold (#ffc60a) or Sunshine (#faa31b) text on White must be avoided since it fails contrast; golds are reserved for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only, never as small body/text color on light backgrounds.

quotes: Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

rules.intent: Ensure information remains accessible to users who cannot distinguish color differences.
rules.rule_text: Color must not be the sole means of conveying meaning; pair color-coded information with icons, depth cues, clear copy, or other visual feedback.
